preamble: The debuccalization of the voiceless uvular fricative [χ] into a glottal stop [ʔ]. This process involves the loss of the oral place of articulation, leaving only the glottal gesture.

examples.note: Documented in several Salishan and Athabaskan languages as part of systematic lenition.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
